findings: The diff includes substantial new test coverage for the pricing logic and default model behavior, and the test summary reports all tests passed.

Test Suggestion 1: Add a boundary test for estimateCost with very large token counts (for example, near Integer.MAX_VALUE) to confirm the double calculation does not overflow or lose precision in a way that changes pricing materially.

Test Suggestion 2: Add a regression test for model-string matching precedence, especially a model name that contains both "4.1" and "mini"-like substrings, to ensure the intended pricing branch is selected and future refactors do not reorder conditions incorrectly.

Test Suggestion 3: Add an integration-style test that verifies the workflow uses AppConfig.DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL when OPENAI_MODEL is unset, and that the resulting cost estimate matches the new gpt-5.4-mini fallback pricing end-to-end.

No security issues detected. The diff updates default LLM model names and pricing logic, adds tests, and enables DUMMY_MODE for Surefire; no secrets, auth bypasses, or unsafe input handling were introduced.

Primary driver: the new PRReviewWorkflowImplTest adds many independent test cases plus repeated branching around model-pricing and aggregation scenarios, increasing decision-path count and mental overhead

P2: The new test class has moderate cyclomatic/cognitive complexity, which makes it harder to maintain and extend [Complexity] - Consider extracting repeated pricing/assertion setup into helper methods or parameterized tests to reduce branching and duplication.

P3: Add a boundary test for very large token counts in estimateCost to confirm double-based calculations remain stable near integer limits [TestQuality] - Use an extreme input case and assert the pricing result stays correct.

P3: Add a regression test for model-string matching precedence when a model name could match multiple branches (e.g., contains both "4.1" and "mini") [TestQuality] - Verify the intended pricing branch is selected and future condition reordering does not change behavior.

P3: Add an integration-style test for the unset OPENAI_MODEL path to verify AppConfig.DEFAULT_OPENAI_MODEL is used end-to-end and maps to the new gpt-5.4-mini fallback pricing [TestQuality] - Cover the full workflow rather than only unit-level cost calculation.

prTitle: Add tests for LLM cost calculation and fix gpt-5.4-mini pricing
author: Copilot
